name: Puerto de Confrides and Coll de Rates Loop
name_original: Los 2000 de La Marina Baja — Circular desde Callosa d'En Sarriá
description: This difficult 58.2-mile (93.7 km) road cycling route from Callosa d'En Sarriá features 6550 feet (1996 metres) of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Callosa D'En Sarrià traverse a landscape defined by dramatic mountain ranges, lush valleys, and well-maintained roads. The region, situated on the Costa Blanca, offers diverse terrain for cyclists. Routes often feature significant elevation gains through terraced hillsides and citrus groves, leading to high mountain passes. This area provides a mix of challenging ascents and scenic rides through varied natural settings. Best road cycling routes around Callosa D'En Sarrià The most popular road cycling route is Puerto de Confrides and Coll de Rates Loop, a 58.2 miles (93.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 18 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant climbs like Coll de Rates, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Marina Baja along the coast — Tour from Altea, a moderate 31.0 miles (50.0 km) path. This route provides coastal views and winds through the lower Marina Baja region. Local road cyclists also love the Sa Creueta Pass (780 m) – Sa Creueta Pass loop from Garganes, a 69.6 miles (112.0 km) trail leading through the challenging Sa Creueta Pass, often completed in about 5 hours 51 minutes. Road cycling around Callosa D'En Sarrià is defined by mountain passes, terraced hillsides, and coastal views. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult ascents. The routes in Callosa D'En Sarrià are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 68000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Callosa D'En Sarrià's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Callosa D'En Sarrià?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes around Callosa D'En Sarrià, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored 58 routes in total.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Callosa D'En Sarrià?
A: The routes in Callosa D'En Sarrià cater to various abilities. You'll find 13 easy routes, 19 moderate routes, and 26 difficult routes, ensuring there's a challenge for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes around Callosa D'En Sarrià?
A: Road cycling routes in this region offer incredibly diverse scenery. You can expect to ride through lush citrus groves, traditional terraced hillsides famous for loquats, and high mountain passes. Many routes also provide stunning coastal views, particularly towards Altea, and tranquil vistas of the Guadalest reservoir surrounded by impressive mountain ranges like Aitana and Bernia.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in Callosa D'En Sarrià?
A: Absolutely. Callosa D'En Sarrià is renowned for its challenging ascents. Cyclists can tackle legendary climbs such as the &lt;strong&gt;Coll de Rates&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Sa Creueta Pass&lt;/strong&gt; (780 m). Other significant climbs include Puerto de Confrides, Alto de Finestrat, Puerto de Tudons, and Port de Bèrnia, offering considerable elevation gains and testing endurance.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are 13 easy road cycling routes available around Callosa D'En Sarrià. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, making them ideal for those new to road cycling or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see along the routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and landmarks. You can encounter the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Rates&lt;/a&gt;, a popular mountain pass, or the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/380855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sa Creueta Pass (780 m)&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/73676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offers picturesque views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/149038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forat de Bèrnia&lt;/a&gt;, a natural tunnel, provides incredible panoramic vistas of the coast and mountains.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Callosa D'En Sarrià?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain passes to scenic coastal roads, and the stunning panoramic views encountered throughout the rides.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e890759210/puerto-de-confrides-and-coll-de-rates-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Confrides and Coll de Rates Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, offering a challenging circular ride with significant climbs.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Callosa D'En Sarrià?
A: The Costa Blanca, including Callosa D'En Sarrià, is renowned as a year-round cycling destination. However, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still enjoying mild weather. Winter is also popular, especially for professional cyclists, due to the generally sunny and temperate conditions.

Q: Can I find routes that offer coastal views?
A: Yes, several routes provide beautiful coastal views. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e892046371/la-marina-baja-along-the-coast-tour-from-altea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Marina Baja along the coast — Tour from Altea&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic stretches along the lower Marina Baja region, with glimpses of the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: For those seeking longer rides, Callosa D'En Sarrià offers routes that can extend over 100 kilometers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32902837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sa Creueta Pass (780 m) – Sa Creueta Pass loop from Garganes&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 112 km route, perfect for long-distance enthusiasts.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the famous loquat orchards?
A: Yes, many routes in the area wind through the traditional terraced hillsides and valleys, where Callosa D'En Sarrià's internationally recognized loquat (níspero) production takes place. These orchards create a unique and picturesque agricultural landscape, especially during the spring bloom.
tips:
The stretch of road between Raco del Fillet and El Castell de Guadalest is a popular route for cyclists due to its stunning scenery and challenging climbs.
It is a route with a fairly steep gradient, making it ideal for cyclists in good physical condition.
Along the route, you can enjoy spectacular views of the valley and the Guadalest reservoir, the route includes roads such as the CV-715 and the CV-70, which are known for their picturesque landscapes and challenging slopes.

One of the most recognized and visited ports of La Marina Baja. With slopes of 6-7% it adapts very well to all profiles of cyclists. Throughout the entire journey you can enjoy beautiful views in any direction you do it. It is usually a regular site for some professionals who use it to train and prepare for the season.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete The 2000 of La Marina Baja route?
faqs.answer: This challenging racebike route is approximately 93.7 kilometers long with nearly 2,000 meters of elevation gain. While the estimated moving time is around 5 hours and 20 minutes, its 'difficult' rating means it often takes cyclists longer, depending on fitness levels and stops. Plan for a full day's adventure.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and a 'Hard road ride' requiring 'very good fitness'. With significant elevation changes, it is not recommended for beginners. Cyclists should have substantial experience with long, demanding climbs and descents.

faqs.question: Is The 2000 of La Marina Baja a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route that conveniently starts and ends in Callosa d'En Sarriá, making logistics straightforward for cyclists.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular loop starting and ending in Callosa d'En Sarriá. As it's a popular cycling hub, you'll typically find public parking available within the town, though specific parking areas are not detailed. Arriving earlier in the day is always a good strategy, especially during peak seasons.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily designed for road cycling, meaning you'll be on paved surfaces. However, given its 'hard' difficulty rating, be prepared for potentially rougher sections or steep climbs that demand good bike handling skills.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle The 2000 of La Marina Baja?
faqs.answer: The Marina Baja region, part of the Costa Blanca, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring (March-May) and Autumn (September-November) are often ideal, with milder temperatures and less intense sun than the summer months. Always check the local weather forecast before you head out.

faqs.question: What are some scenic highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning vistas characteristic of the Marina Baja region. You'll experience a Winding Road Through Pine Forests (CV-715 and CV-70, Guadalest Valley) and conquer the renowned Coll de Rates Pass, both offering memorable views and challenging climbs.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other notable cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find yourself on sections of the [CIMA AT04] Confrides (puerto de Ares), Vall d'Alcalà Pla de Petracos, and La Vall de Laguar-La Vuelta al Cavall Verd, among others, for short stretches.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is a demanding road cycling trail. While there are no specific restrictions against dogs, it is generally not suitable for cycling with pets due to the speed, distance, and elevation changes involved. It's best to leave your furry friends at home for this particular adventure.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route. However, always respect local traffic laws and any signage you encounter.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this challenging ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and significant elevation, you should bring a well-maintained road bike, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, plenty of water and nutrition, a repair kit (spare tubes, pump, multi-tool), and a fully charged phone. Consider a GPS device or the komoot app for navigation, as the route covers a substantial distance.

waypoints.name: Winding Road Through Pine Forests (CV-715 and CV-70, Guadalest Valley)
